volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 4.4.581b - Section 4.4.630b)

sections.label: Section 4.4.581b
sections.original: Tam quam di omnes qui caelum colunt .
sections.translation: In the same measure as all the Divinities who inhabit the heavens .

sections.label: Section 4.4.582
sections.original: Tum tu mi es inimicus certus . nam generi lenonio
sections.translation: Then you are an assured enemy to me ; for to the race of procurers no God was ever so kind as to prove propitious .

sections.label: Section 4.4.584
sections.original: Hoc age . opusnest hac tibi empta ?
sections.translation: Attend to the business in hand . Have you any need to purchase her ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.584b
sections.translation: If you have need for her to be sold ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.585
sections.original: mihi quoque emptast ; si tibi subiti nihil est , tantumdemst mihi .
sections.translation: I , too , have need to purchase her ; if you have no sudden occasion to sell , just in the same degree have I to buy .

sections.label: Section 4.4.586
sections.translation: State a sum ; name a price .

sections.label: Section 4.4.586b
sections.translation: The commodity is your own ; it ’ s for you to name a sum .

sections.label: Section 4.4.587
sections.translation: He asks what ’ s right .

sections.label: Section 4.4.587b
sections.translation: Do you wish to buy at a bargain ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.587c
sections.translation: Do you wish to sell at a handsome profit ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.588
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m sure that both of you would like to do so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.588b
sections.translation: Come , boldly name your price .

sections.label: Section 4.4.589
sections.original: Prius dico : hanc mancupio nemo tibi dabit . iam scis ?
sections.translation: I tell you beforehand ; no one will dispose of her to you on warranty . Do you so understand it , then ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.589b
sections.translation: I understand it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.590
sections.original: indica , minimo daturus qui sis , qui duci queat .
sections.translation: Declare what ’ s the lowest price at which you ’ ll offer her , for which she may be taken by the purchaser .

sections.label: Section 4.4.591
sections.original: Tace , tace . nimis tú quidem hercle homo stultus es pueriliter .
sections.translation: Hold your tongue , hold your tongue . Really , upon my faith , you are a very simple man , with your childish ways .

sections.label: Section 4.4.592
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.592b
sections.original: Quia enim te ex puella prius percontari volo
sections.translation: Why because I wish you first to make enquiries of the damsel which relate to your interest .

sections.label: Section 4.4.593b
sections.original: Atque hercle tu me monuisti hau male .
sections.translation: And really , upon my faith , you ’ ve given me no bad advice . Look at that , will you . I , an experienced Procurer , had almost fallen into the pit ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.595
sections.original: ni hic adesses . quantum est adhibere hominem amicum , ubi quid geras .
sections.translation: if you had not been here . How important a point it is to have a person your friend at hand when you are about anything .

sections.label: Section 4.4.596
sections.original: Quo genere aut qua in patria nata sit aut quibus parentibus ,
sections.translation: I want you to make enquiry of her , of what family or in what country she was born , or of what parents , so that you mayn ’ t say that you ’ ve bought her at hazard by my persuasion or suggestion .

sections.label: Section 4.4.598b
sections.translation: On the contrary , I approve of your counsel , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.599
sections.original: Nisi molestum est , percontari hanc paucis hic volt .
sections.translation: Unless it ’ s troublesome , he ’ s desirous to make a few enquiries of her .

sections.label: Section 4.4.599b
sections.translation: By all means ;

sections.label: Section 4.4.600
sections.translation: at his own pleasure .

sections.label: Section 4.4.600b
sections.original: Quid stas ? adi sis tute atque ipse itidem roga ,
sections.translation: Why do you delay ? Go to him yourself ; and do you yourself ask him as well , that you may be allowed to make such enquiries as you please ; although he has told me that he gives permission to do so of her , still I had rather that you yourself should go to him , that he mayn ’ t be holding you in contempt .

sections.label: Section 4.4.603b
sections.translation: You give me very proper advice . ( Accosting SAGARISTIO .) Stranger , I should like to ask some questions of her .

sections.label: Section 4.4.604b
sections.original: A terra ad caelum , quid lubet .
sections.translation: From earth to heaven , whatever you like .

sections.label: Section 4.4.605
sections.original: Iube dum eam hoc accedat ad me .
sections.translation: Just bid her to step this way to me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.605b
sections.original: I sane ac morem illi gere .
sections.translation: Go you , then , and humour him . ( To DORDALUS .) Make enquiry , question her , just as you please .

sections.label: Section 4.4.606b
sections.original: Age , age nunc tu , in proelium
sections.translation: Well , well [*] , get on then ; make your preparations . ( Aside to the YOUNG WOMAN .) Take you care to commence with a good omen .

sections.label: Section 4.4.607b
sections.translation: The auspices are favourable .

sections.label: Section 4.4.609
sections.translation: Hold your tongue . [*] ( To DORDALUS .) Step you aside here ; I will now conduct her to you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.609b
sections.original: Age , ut rem esse in nostram putas .
sections.translation: Do what you think is most for my interest .

sections.label: Section 4.4.611
sections.original: Sequere me . adduco hanc , si quid vis ex hac percontarier .
sections.translation: Follow me . ( To DORDALUS .) I ’ ve brought her , if you are wishful to make any enquiries of her .

sections.label: Section 4.4.612
sections.translation: But I want you to be present .

sections.label: Section 4.4.612b
sections.original: Hau possum quin huic operam dem hospiti ,
sections.translation: I cannot do otherwise than pay attention to this stranger ( pointing to SAGARISTIO .) whom my master bade me show courtesy to . What if he doesn ’ t choose that I should be present together with you ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.613b
sections.translation: Yes , but do come .

sections.label: Section 4.4.614
sections.translation: I ’ ll lend you my assistance , then .

sections.label: Section 4.4.614b
sections.original: Tibi ibidem das , ubi tu tuom amicum adiuvas .
sections.translation: You ’ re lending it yourself as well when you are assisting your friend .

sections.label: Section 4.4.615
sections.translation: Examine her . ( Aside to the YOUNG WOMAN .) Hark you , be on your guard .

sections.label: Section 4.4.615b
sections.original: Satis est dictum : quamquam ego serva sum ,
sections.translation: Enough has been said to me . ( Aloud .) Although I am a slave , I know my duty , so that whatever he asks I ’ ll tell the truth as I have heard it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.617
sections.translation: Young woman , this is an honorable man .

sections.label: Section 4.4.617b
sections.translation: I believe you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.617c
sections.translation: You ’ ll not be long in servitude with him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.618
sections.original: Ita pol spero , si parentes facient officium suom .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , and so I trust , if my parents do their duty .

sections.label: Section 4.4.619
sections.original: Nolo ego te mirari , si nos ex te percontabimur
sections.translation: I do not wish you to be surprised , if we make enquiries of you

sections.label: Section 4.4.620
sections.translation: about either your country or your parents .

sections.label: Section 4.4.620b
sections.original: Quor ego id mirer , mi homo ?
sections.translation: Why should I be surprised at that , my dear sir ? My state of servitude has forbidden me to be surprised at any misfortune of my own .

sections.label: Section 4.4.622b
sections.original: Ah , di istam perdant , ita catast et callida .
sections.translation: May the Gods confound her ! so cunning and crafty is she . She has got shrewd sense : how readily she does say what ’ s needed .

sections.label: Section 4.4.623b
sections.translation: What ’ s your name ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.624
sections.translation: Now I ’ m afraid she ’ ll be tripping .

sections.label: Section 4.4.624b
sections.translation: My name was Lucris [*] in my own country .

sections.label: Section 4.4.625
sections.original: Nomen atque omen quantivis iam est preti . quin tu hanc emis ?
sections.translation: The name and the omen are worth any price . Why don ’ t you make purchase of her ? ( Aside .) I was greatly afraid that she would be tripping . She has got herself free .

sections.label: Section 4.4.626b
sections.translation: If I make purchase of you , I trust that you ’ ll prove Lucris to myself as well .

sections.label: Section 4.4.627b
sections.translation: If you do make purchase of her , never , on my word , do I think that she ’ ll remain your slave throughout the month .

sections.label: Section 4.4.629
sections.translation: And so indeed I ’ d hope , i ’ faith .

sections.label: Section 4.4.629b
sections.translation: That what you wish may come to pass , employ your own energies .

sections.label: Section 4.4.630
sections.translation: ( Aside .) In nothing even as yet has she made a slip .

sections.label: Section 4.4.630b
sections.translation: Where were you born ?
